comment.body: Why don’t you wait for tomorrow’s data release? If the market is falling before the open, it’ll likely keep falling after the open and you can cash in, and then buy the dip. If the market isn’t falling, you won’t be able to close. We short futures with stop-losses. Use options only once the move strengthens. Options are a leveraged instrument

question on most folks minds. There is wide consensus that print is expected to come in hot. This is one of the reasons they announced the china extension by 90 days and Treasury has a huge buyback to calm yields tomorrow. Any fall might be brief premarket and maybe at open they rip it up. 

Even if a fall occurs, will be contained is my simplistic guess.

comment.body: We, along with other funds, are waiting for both the CPI and PPI releases and the Putin–Trump summit to see how global trade shapes up, and we’ll rebalance our portfolios accordingly. The problem is that inflation swaps are pricing higher inflation over the next 12–24 months..even household inflation expectations. The 1yr inflation swap is at 3.4%!! Obviously many of you know the Fed can steer short-term yields.. The longer end is harder. For now, this isn’t an environment for long-term allocations, it’s mostly intraday scalping and swing trading. Interests are shifting, companies are restructuring, competition is intense, and the weight and value of different players are very fluid. Longer-term commitment in this market isn’t really feasible right now—except in three sectors, which I won’t get into here. We receive front-end ZCIS and pay 5–10y (playing the down-sloping inflation curve), while owning convexity for this week

comment.body:
Alma didn’t write much about this..she only hinted i think, but it’s important whether the Fed cuts because inflation risk is easing or because recession risk has spiked. In the latter case, rate cuts won’t help because of tariffs, and that would be the most bearish QE you’ve probably ever seen.
This is especially important for those planning long term. It’s no accident there’s no clear signal from alma or anyone else. In times like this, most money flows into precious metals and bonds. US equity outflow is high

The problem is that inflation swaps are pricing higher inflation over the next 12–24 months..even household inflation expectations. The 1yr inflation swap is at 3.4%!! Obviously many of you know the Fed can steer short-term yields.. The longer end is harder. For now, this isn’t an environment for long-term allocations, it’s mostly intraday scalping and swing trading. Interests are shifting, companies are restructuring, competition is intense, and the weight and value of different players are very fluid. Longer-term commitment in this market isn’t really feasible right now—except in three sectors, which I won’t get into here.
